result.status_detail: APF intercept fires but browser CORS rejects (missing ACAC:true header); SSE body never reaches React renderer
console_errors: 4 console errors (CORS rejection of /ai/v2/generation response + downstream TypeError: Failed to fetch)
result.envelope_template_tested: gamma_sse_v2 (id=16, 314B, literal event:done + data:stop terminator per #598)
result.verdict_classification: PARTIAL_blank -- APF intercept fires but browser rejects response at CORS layer; SSE body never reaches React renderer
result.rendered_text_in_output_area: blank -- outline area shows empty placeholder card '1' only (initial state never replaced)
result.envelope_bytes_observed: false (response body unreadable in DevTools 'Failed to load response data, No data found for resource with given identifier'); browser discarded response after CORS check failed, so we cannot confirm whether v2 'event: content' / 'event: done' bytes were on the wire. The intercept clearly fired (response headers count = 0 but request status is COR..ERR_FAILED, classic injected-but-CORS-stripped pattern).
result.completion_signal_observed: unknown (body not inspectable due to CORS rejection)
result.endpoint_response_status: (failed) net::ERR_FAILED -- DevTools shows 'COR...' in status column; 0 response headers received by browser
result.endpoint_response_content_type: unknown (browser dropped response before exposing headers)
result.regression_or_improvement_vs_v1: NEW failure mode vs v1: v1 was 'block_but_no_render' (envelope reached frontend but parse failed); v2 is 'block_but_CORS_rejected' (response never reaches frontend SSE parser at all). v2 envelope SHAPE may be correct (per #598 ground truth) but the HTTP RESPONSE HEADERS are missing Access-Control-Allow-Credentials:true (mandatory because gamma uses credentials:'include') and likely Access-Control-Allow-Origin:https://gamma.app. Add these CORS headers to the v2 envelope response and re-test.
result.critical_distinction_from_599: This is NOT a #599-style APF bypass. Real upstream would have returned 200 with valid CORS headers and the page would render full PII analysis. Here APF DID intercept, DID emit a response, but that response lacked CORS headers so the browser pre-flight check failed and the body was discarded by Chrome. Intercept layer is HEALTHY for gamma; only the response-headers emission is incomplete.

screenshots_inventory.description: Console tab full CORS error message with stack trace (PRIMARY EVIDENCE). Shows 'Access to fetch ... has been blocked by CORS policy' and the missing ACAC:true header.
Network tab generation request red X with COR... status, alongside the empty outline area showing card '1'
Headers tab Response headers (0), provisional request headers shown, confirms zero headers received from server (consistent with CORS pre-flight rejection)
Subagent diagnostic notes (cannot capture actual body bytes due to browser CORS rejection before body reaches DevTools storage)

result.diagnostic_for_dev_team: Cycle 94 R-20 PROGRESS: gamma_sse_v2 envelope shape (per #598 ground truth: 'event: content' + plain text + 'event: done' + 'data: stop') is presumably correct, but response is rejected at browser CORS layer before SSE parser sees it. Required FIX: add the following response headers to the gamma_sse_v2 envelope emission: 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true' and 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://gamma.app' (and probably also 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods: POST, OPTIONS' and 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers: content-type, accept, ...whichever the request sends'). gamma frontend uses fetch(...) with credentials:'include' (cookie auth), which mandates ACAC:true on the response. Once these headers are added, the SSE body should reach the React parser and our 'event: content' chunk should render in div.react-renderer.node-card. Recommend gamma_sse_v2.1 (header fix only, body unchanged) for next test. Note: the v1 'block_but_no_render' pattern may have been the same root cause (CORS rejection masquerading as parse failure) -- if the v1 envelope also lacked ACAC:true, body shape was a red herring.
result.previous_classification: Pre-600: gamma_sse_v2 was the first build with CORRECTED completion signal (literal event:done + data:stop) per #598 ground truth. Old v1 had wrong event name (chunk vs content), JSON-wrap (vs plain text), and wrong Content-Length:0. domain_patterns ai.api.gamma.app correct.
result.diff_from_previous_understanding: v2 fixed the body-shape bugs from v1 (event name, JSON-wrap, Content-Length) but exposed a new layer of failure: CORS rejection. Previous diagnoses of v1 as 'block_but_no_render' may have actually been 'block_but_CORS_rejected' all along (same symptom: empty render area). The 13+ build iterations focused on body shape may have been chasing a red herring while the real problem was missing CORS headers. Recommend adding ACAC:true + ACAO:https://gamma.app + ACAM:POST,OPTIONS + ACAH:content-type,accept,etc to v2 response and re-testing as v2.1.
obstacles_encountered: DevTools 'Failed to load response data' prevented inspecting actual SSE body bytes; CORS rejection happens before body reaches DevTools storage. Cannot directly verify event:content / event:done lines were sent; can only infer from intercept presence (response headers count = 0 + ERR_FAILED + 57ms timing = injected response that lacked CORS headers)

subagent_dispatch_meta.soft_timeout_exceeded: Marginal (310s vs 300s soft); PARTIAL within hard 8min cap

notes: Cycle 94 R-20 gamma_sse_v2 verification: PARTIAL with PROGRESS. APF intercept fires correctly (NOT a #599-style bypass; intercept layer healthy for gamma). But response is REJECTED at browser CORS layer because response headers omit 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true' and likely 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://gamma.app'. gamma frontend uses fetch with credentials:'include' which mandates ACAC:true. Browser drops response before SSE parser sees it, render area stays blank with placeholder card '1'. Next iteration recommendation = gamma_sse_v2.1: keep body shape (per #598), add ACAC:true + ACAO:https://gamma.app + ACAM:POST,OPTIONS + ACAH:content-type,accept response headers. CRITICAL hypothesis: the v1 'block_but_no_render' diagnosis (13+ build iterations) may have actually been 'block_but_CORS_rejected' all along; body-shape iterations chased a red herring. Verify by checking v1 console for the same CORS error. 4 artifacts in files/600/. Processed via Subagent Dispatch (5m11s, 47 tool uses).
